concept: Accessible / Practically Available to a Stated Person and Activity
description: Having conditions under which a stated person can reach, understand, use, or participate in something. The adjective is relational. A route may be accessible for one journey and fail another. 'kelasu' (usable) describes whether something works once it is available; 'lokane' also asks whether the person can get to that use.
sound_symbolism: Low liquid 'lo' releases the tongue into round 'o'. Stressed 'ka' places a clean velar contact in the middle and opens through broad 'a'. Final 'ne' settles at the teeth with a soft nasal and balanced 'e'. Flowing openness passes through one firm point and reaches a quiet resting place.
grammatical_notes: A predicative or attributive adjective: 'ha shoeka lokane nai.' (This route is accessible.) The surrounding clause can name the person, activity, conditions, and time for which the claim holds. The quality-noun rule supplies accessibility as a stated condition.

pillars.solarpunk-values: A place built for shared life must be judged where bodies, tools, routes, and activities actually meet. A drawing alone cannot settle the question.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: A relational adjective leaves speakers room to correct a broad accessibility claim with the person and activity it overlooked.

tags.activity: practical availability for a stated use
tags.community: conditions that permit participation in shared life
tags.spatial: a route or place reachable under stated conditions
